# Generated by ollama (codellama)
# Prompt: Create a multi-job GitHub Actions workflow where one job builds artifacts and subsequent jobs download and deploy them. The deployment job should download artifacts from the build job and execute deployment scripts. This is for our multi-stage deployment pipeline.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Deployment Pipeline

on: [push]

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2
      - name: Build artifacts
        run: |
          npm install
          npm run build
      - name: Upload artifacts to GitHub Actions
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v2
        with:
          name: artifacts
          path: ./dist

  download_artifacts:
    needs: build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2
      - name: Download artifacts from GitHub Actions
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v2
        with:
          name: artifacts
  deploy:
    needs: download_artifacts
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2
      - name: Download artifacts from GitHub Actions
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v2
        with:
          name: artifacts
      - name: Execute deployment scripts
        run: |
          npm install
          npm run deploy
```
This workflow defines three jobs: `build`, `download_artifacts`, and `deploy`. The `build` job builds the project using `npm run build`, and uploads the artifacts to GitHub Actions using the `actions/upload-artifact@v2` action. The `download_artifacts` job downloads the artifacts from GitHub Actions using the `actions/download-artifact@v2` action, and the `deploy` job downloads the artifacts and executes deployment scripts using `npm run deploy`.

The workflow is triggered on push events to any branch in the repository, and runs on an Ubuntu runner. The `needs` keyword is used to specify that the `download_artifacts` job needs to complete successfully before the `deploy` job can start running.
